title: Three wave mixing vacuum squeezing generation in a SNAIL-based Traveling-Wave Parametric Amplifier with alternated flux polarity
authors: Isita Chatterjee, Pegah Darvehi, Antonio Orsi, Anna Levochkina, Pasquale Mastrovito, Gwenel Le Gal, Arpit Ranadive, Giulio Cappelli, Alberto Porzio, Francesco Tafuri, Davide Massarotti, Martina Esposito
abstract: Recent demonstrations of squeezing generation using Traveling Wave Parametric Amplifiers (TWPAs) have opened the way for the application of broadband microwave squeezing in quantum sensing, quantum-enhanced detection, and continuous-variable quantum information. Here we demonstrate vacuum squeezing generation via residual three-wave mixing (3WM) in a Josephson TWPA based on superconducting nonlinear asymmetric inductive elements (SNAILs) with alternated magnetic flux polarity. By investigating competition between four-wave mixing (4WM) and 3WM nonlinearities, we prove that vacuum squeezing generation via residual 3WM is possible when a careful choice of the operating flux point is adopted. Our study provides valuable insights on the impact of competing nonlinearities on TWPA squeezers, potentially extending the range of applications in the framework of microwave photonics.
summary: The researchers successfully demonstrated vacuum squeezing and entanglement generation in a SNAIL-based traveling-wave parametric amplifier by using flux tuning to suppress unwanted four-wave mixing. This approach allows for effective three-wave mixing, which simplifies the separation of pump and signal frequencies. The results provide a pathway for advancing broadband microwave squeezing for quantum sensing and information processing.
main_problem: Investigating how competing three-wave mixing (3WM) and four-wave mixing (4WM) nonlinearities affect vacuum squeezing generation in a SNAIL-based Traveling-Wave Parametric Amplifier (TWPA).
main_result: Demonstrated that residual 3WM can be used for squeezing generation by optimizing the external magnetic flux to suppress 4WM, achieving single-mode squeezing of ~3 dB and generating entanglement.
method: Experimental measurement of idler power spectral density and phase-dependent gain using a CW pump, combined with rigorous system gain calibration via a shot noise tunnel junction (SNTJ) and WRSPICE simulations.
model_or_system: A Josephson Traveling-Wave Parametric Amplifier (JTWPA) consisting of 700 SNAIL unit cells with alternated magnetic flux polarity, operating in a superconducting circuit architecture.
key_observables: Idler power spectral density (PSD), phase-dependent signal gain, field quadrature variances (X and P), single-mode squeezing (SMS), and logarithmic negativity (E_N).
important_parameters: Pump frequency of 7.705 GHz, external magnetic flux (optimized at 0.59 Phi_0), and pump powers between -84 dBm and -91 dBm.
main_assumptions: Assumes a 1 dB upper bound uncertainty for insertion loss and system gain estimation, leading to a conservative lower bound for reported squeezing and entanglement.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Device schematic and flux dependence of nonlinear coefficients; Fig 3: Comparison of squeezing/entanglement at different flux points; Fig 6: Experimental setup schematic; Fig 7: PSD as a function of SNTJ voltage for gain calibration.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the device physics of SNAIL-based TWPAs, details the experimental setup and calibration procedures, compares 3WM and 4WM regimes via flux tuning, and concludes with the demonstration of squeezing and entanglement.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to quantum optics and continuous-variable quantum information as it demonstrates a method to utilize residual nonlinearities for broadband squeezing and provides a strategy for easier pump filtering in microwave photonics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Device sketch and SNAIL flux tunability. (a) Circuit schematic of three neighboring unit cells in a SNAIL- based JTWPA device with alternated magnetic flux polarity. (b) 3WM coefficient β and 4WM coefficient γ for one SNAIL as a function of external magnetic flux.

Figure 2. Idler generation in 3WM and 4WM. (a) Sketch of the frequency configuration for 3WM and 4WM experiments where ∆= 31 MHz. (b) Experimental results for 3WM and 4WM idler measured power referred at TWPA output vs flux; pump frequency fp = 7.705 GHz, pump power at device input Pp = −87 dBm, signal power at device input Ps = −120 dBm. (c) Results of WRSpice numerical simula- tions (see Appendix C). The vertical dashed and dotted lines indicate the selected flux points.

Figure 3. Degenerate gain and single mode squeezing for Φ1 (left) and Φ2 (right). (a-b) 3WM degenerate gain vs pump phase, pump frequency fp = 7.705 GHz, pump power at TWPA input Pp = −84 dBm for Φ1 and Pp = −91 dBm for Φ2. (c-d) 3WM single-mode squeezing values, Sx and Sp, along the x and p quadratures as a function of pump phase; number of repeated acquisitions for each phase, Nrep = 3 × 106 for Φ1 and Nrep = 1 × 106 for Φ2; integration time for each acquisition, 10 µs. (e, g) Examples of single-mode differential (pump ON - pump OFF) phase space histogram plots for Nrep = 8 × 106

Figure 4. Non-degenerate gain and entanglement verification in two-mode squeezing for Φ1 (left) and Φ2 (right). (a-b) 3WM signal and idler gain experimental results versus pump power at TWPA input; the insets are zoom in of the results; pump frequency fp = 7.705 GHz; detuning ∆= 31 MHz for Φ1 and ∆= 61 MHz for Φ2. (c-d) Logarithmic negativity as a function of pump power; number of repeated acquisitions Nrep = 1 × 106, integration time for each acquisition 10 µs.

Figure 5. Examples of two mode squeezing experimental results. (a) Differential (pump ON - pump OFF) phase space histogram plots for Nrep = 8 × 106 acquisitions with integration time 10 µs. Flux operating point Φ1, pump frequency fp = 7.705 GHz, detuning ∆= 31 MHz, pump power at TWPA input Pp = −83 dBm. (b) Corresponding inferred covariance matrix with uncertainty signified by shaded region.
